provenance.source_corpus: Carbon and Nitrogen Mineralization and Microbial Succession in Oil Sands Reclamation Soils Amended with Pyrogenic Carbon

source_spans.text: Results indicated that the different PyC types increased C mineralization compared to the control
Results indicated that the different PyC types increased C mineralization compared to the control
In contrast, PyC caused a decrease in N mineralization
Molecular sequencing of bacterial DNA showed that PyC, except for pet-coke, increased diversity in both the FFM and NFS
Ordination of distances between community structures based on θyc showed both charcoal and biochar treatments shift the bacterial community structure of FFM and NFS
The exception to that is the fungal community diversity from FFM which drops in response to biochar
Ordination of distances between community structures based on Jclass showed that only the NFS community responded to the biochar treatment
In contrast to charcoal and biochar amendments, petroleum coke addition to soil lowers levels of C mineralization
The exception to that is the fungal community diversity from FFM which drops in response to biochar
